design_philosophy.player_fantasy: Embody Judge Dredd enforcing law in a dystopian future setting based on the 1995 film

difficulty.player_agency_in_difficulty: Cheat codes available enabling infinite health mode, suggesting potential for high baseline difficulty

progression.unlock_mechanism: Level select available via cheat code activation
replayability_hooks: Hidden developer message and credits discoverable through ROM exploration
Cheat modes suggesting developer acknowledgment of difficulty scaling

world_building_techniques: Licensed film IP (Judge Dredd 1995) providing established universe
Character-driven action gameplay rooted in dystopian sci-fi setting

faction_indicators: Judge Dredd as lawman authority figure
Dystopian future setting implying social hierarchy
danger_signals: Crime-ridden environments suggested by action-game premise
environmental_storytelling: Film-licensed setting providing pre-established visual identity
Action gameplay framing law enforcement operations in hostile environments

sources_used: tcrf - The Cutting Room Floor Judge Dredd (SNES) article
